hypothesis: The current structural marker smoke test hits only 3 URLs (homepage, /products/, /services/). A single-product template regression or category archive collapse would pass the gate undetected. Expanding the smoke URL set from 3 to 6 — adding a live single-product URL, a service-detail URL, and a product category archive URL — will catch template-specific regressions on a broader set of WP template files before they reach CI. Basis: both prior keeps (php -l, marker check) targeted identified failure classes with surgical gates; this extends the proven marker-check approach to template types that appeared in the historical failure record (product taxonomy seed failure mapped to product archive templates; homepage-parity banner mapped to homepage template). Adding 3 representative pages triples per-push template coverage at negligible curl cost.
learning: 23 CI runs in window (2026-04-29T06:56:46Z → 2026-05-01T14:44 UTC), all 23 success (100% first-push pass rate). Expanded URL set (6 vs 3) covered single-product, service-detail, and category archive templates on every push. Zero failures in 23 runs across high-churn period (hero image iterations, brand-page restructure, product taxonomy work). Gate is functioning — 100% maintained. KEEP.
changes_description: Expanded structural marker smoke URL set from 3 to 6: added single-product URL (?p=368), service-detail URL (/service/marine/), and product category archive (/product-category/outboard-motors/) to SMOKE_URLS array. All 4 REQUIRED_MARKERS unchanged. Targets template-specific regressions on WP template types not covered by the prior 3-URL set.
